Ntc Of Mtn For Clarification Of Order Re: Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Dec-19-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Air Systems Acquisition, Inc.,'s Motion For Clarification Of Order re: Motion To Compel is granted as it seeks a verification under penalty of perjury that all responsive documents have been produced. While a privilege log generally is not verified as a stand alone document, it is included in the verification that all responsive documents have been produced or identified.
As the motion seeks a finding that documents that were not communicated to Plaintiff must be included in the privilege log, the motion is denied.
The inquiry ruled upon in the prior motion went to what Gilbane's counsel communicated or did not communicate to Gilbane. Any documents that were provided/communicated to Gilbane fall within the category of documents that must be listed in the privilege log. However, if they were not communicated to Gilbane, they do not fall within the category of documents which were sought or ruled upon. They do not have to be identified in a privilege log. The attorneys' files which were not communicated to Plaintiff do not fall within the area of proper inquiry. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Deposition Questions
Off Calendar Nov-20-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Air Systems Acquisition, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; and Deposition Questions is denied. While the [party's] knowledge is discoverable, "... the mere fact that [the party's] state of mind is in issue does not cause a waiver of [the party's] privilege concerning communications between [the party] and [the party's] attorneys...." Miller v Superior Court, 111 Cal.App.3d 390, 395.
The question at deposition that is at issue seeks a response about what Gilbane's counsel did or did not communicate to Gilbane. That invades Gilbane's privilege. The document request seeks documents prepared by Gilbane's counsel in the course of its investigation of the licensure issue. Those documents constitute counsel's legal opinion and advice given and are privileged.
The court does recognize that there is a possibility that Plaintiff has not produced a full privilege log setting forth all responsive documents. Plaintiff is therefore ordered to supplement its privilege log or to verify that all responsive documents have been identified in the log that has been produced. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
